title: The First Tome: On the First Death
lore: Nothing here dies only once. The first creature to fall learned that the dark does not keep what it takes — it returns it, smaller, hungrier, and wiser. They named this turning the cycle of rebirth.
deep: Each rebirth strips the flesh but leaves a scar of memory in the soul. That scar is not weakness; it is the ledger of every death, and the System reads it to decide what you may become.

title: On Wounds and Mending
lore: Flesh torn in the dark does not wait for kindness. Those who survive learn to knit themselves shut between heartbeats — to heal is the first skill of the deep.
deep: Regeneration is not free; it borrows from tomorrow's strength. Push it past its limit and the body forgets how to stop closing, sealing even what should stay open.

title: The Weaver's Patience
lore: The strongest hunter never chases. It waits at the center of a trap it spun in silence, and lets the careless deliver themselves.
deep: Every thread is a question the prey answers with its own weight. Mastery is not in the strike but in the stillness that makes the strike unnecessary.

title: Hunger, the Silent Court
lore: The dungeon judges no one. Only hunger holds court here, and it rules the patient and the proud alike — wait too long and it collects its tax in flesh.
deep: To eat your own kind quiets the hunger but stains the ruler within. Gluttony is the throne hunger offers to those who stop saying no.
